libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, external service, environment variable, or system property is indicated. OSGi coverage can be exercised with in-process Bundle/BundleContext test doubles rather than a real OSGi container.

deterministic_setup.reason: com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14 references org.osgi.framework.BundleReference and FrameworkUtil from the provided OSGi Core 4.2.0 API; without it, OSGi-related tests fail at runtime before meaningful coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8875 [Automation] java run fails for com.sun.jersey:jersey-core:1.14; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The carried-forward 1.9 scaffold already declares org.osgi:osgi_R4_core:1.0; appending org.osgi.core:4.2.0 may leave duplicate org.osgi.framework classes on the classpath, so local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative.
If org.osgi.core:4.2.0 shadows the older API during compilation, existing OSGi test doubles may need to implement additional interface methods.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jersey Core 1.14 declares OSGi Core 4.2.0 as a provided dependency and its OSGi registry references classes absent from the older osgi_R4_core API used by the current scaffold.
